name: Country-Fried Steak
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium mixing bowl, beat eggs and 2/3 cup milk. Whisk in 3/4 cup flour and 1 teaspoon salt.
2. Place cracker crumbs in a soup plate or pie pan.
3. Dip steaks first into the egg mixture, then into the cracker crumbs. Press crumbs onto steaks.
4. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a 12-inch skillet over medium-low heat.
5. Place prepared steaks in the heated skillet and fry for 6 minutes on one side.
6. Add remaining 2 tablespoons oil, and fry for another 6 minutes on the other side.
7. Remove steaks from the pan using a spatula to avoid tearing the batter.
8. Prepare gravy by stirring flour into drippings, adding oil to wet the flour, then whisking in milk until fully mixed. Add onions and cook for 1 minute after the milk boils. Stir in salt and pepper.
9. Return steaks to the pan and simmer over very low heat for an additional 20 minutes.
10. Serve with gravy spooned over the steaks.
Makes 4 servings.
